description:
A developer just inherited a legacy project whose documentation files are still stored in the single-byte ISO-8859-1 encoding.  
    Your job is to modernise the project by converting every text file under  

        /home/user/project/docs  

    from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8 and to record what was done.

    Detailed requirements that **the automated grader will check**:

    1. Encoding conversion  
       • Only files whose names end in “.txt” and are **directly** inside  /home/user/project/docs (no sub-directories) must be converted.  
       • The conversion must be in-place: after you finish, the original file paths must still exist, but their byte-stream encoding must be UTF-8.  
       • The textual content must stay identical (only the encoding changes).

    2. Verification log  
       • After all conversions create the log file  
             /home/user/project/encoding_conversion.log  
       • The log is a single, plain-text CSV file with **Unix line-feeds (LF)**.  
       • Its exact format:  
             filename,previous_encoding,new_encoding  
             <file1>,ISO-8859-1,UTF-8  
             <file2>,ISO-8859-1,UTF-8  
             …  
         a) The first line is the header exactly as written above.  
         b) Each subsequent line lists one converted file.  
         c) “filename” means *just* the basename (e.g. “readme.txt”), **not** the full path.  
         d) Lines must be sorted alphabetically by filename.  
         e) There must be **no extra columns, no extra whitespace, and no trailing comma** on any line.  

    3. What the grader will test  
       • Every “.txt” file that originally existed in /home/user/project/docs is now UTF-8 encoded (the grader will inspect the MIME type or run “file -bi”).  
       • The log file exists in exactly the path shown and matches the format above, listing each converted file once and only once.  

    Perform the task entirely from the command line; do not delete or rename any files and do not create additional files outside the specified log.  
    When you are finished, the developer should be able to open everything in any modern editor without garbled characters and see a perfectly formatted conversion log.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent runs):

    Directory /home/user/project/docs contains exactly three ISO-8859-1 encoded files:

    1. /home/user/project/docs/readme.txt  
       Text (in ISO-8859-1 bytes):  
           "Curaçao project documentation  
            ============================  
            Version 1.0 – août 2023  
            …"

    2. /home/user/project/docs/changelog.txt  
       Text (in ISO-8859-1 bytes):  
           "Changelog  
            ---------  
            * 2023-08-15 : Première version publiquée  
            * 2023-09-01 : Corrections mineures  
            "

    3. /home/user/project/docs/license.txt  
       Text (in ISO-8859-1 bytes):  
           "Licence MIT  
            ------------  
            Copyright © 2023 ACME Corp.  
            Permission est accordée, gratuitement, à toute personne obtenant une copie…  
            "

    No other “.txt” files exist anywhere inside /home/user/project/docs, and there is no file named /home/user/project/encoding_conversion.log yet.

    Expected final state (after the agent succeeds):

    • The three files listed above are still present at the same paths but their byte encoding has changed to UTF-8.  
    • Their textual content is byte-for-byte identical when interpreted as characters (accented characters now occupy multi-byte UTF-8 sequences).  
    • A new file /home/user/project/encoding_conversion.log exists with the exact content (LF line endings):

        filename,previous_encoding,new_encoding
        changelog.txt,ISO-8859-1,UTF-8
        license.txt,ISO-8859-1,UTF-8
        readme.txt,ISO-8859-1,UTF-8

    • No extra files were created and no filenames were changed.
